title: Avoid multiple break or continue statements in a loop
description: Multiple break or continue statements in a loop can increase complexity and make it harder to understand the program logic. This rule reports an issue when there is more than one break or continue statement in a loop. The code should be refactored to increase readability if there is more than one.
recommendation: Refactor the code to have only one break or continue statement per loop

checks.message: This loop contains more than a single break or continue statement. The first unconditional break or continue statement renders the ones that come after unreachable
